vocab.meaning: chuẩn bị tài chính cho tương lai (thường là làm giàu cho bản thân)
vocab.example: Many people use high-interest savings accounts as a way to feather their nest for retirement.

vocab.meaning: một khoản tiền tiết kiệm để dành cho tương lai
vocab.example: Banks are the primary place where individuals build up a nest egg to buy a house or fund their children's education.

vocab.meaning: tiền dự phòng cho những lúc khó khăn/khẩn cấp
vocab.example: It is common practice to keep some money for a rainy day in a separate bank account that isn't easily accessible.

vocab.meaning: chi tiêu quá nhiều/tốn quá nhiều tiền
vocab.example: People often apply for personal loans because they want to renovate their homes without having to break the bank all at once.

vocab.meaning: tình trạng nợ nần/tài khoản bị âm tiền
vocab.example: Banks provide overdraft facilities that allow businesses to operate temporarily even when they are in the red.
For those who bring home the bacon, direct deposit into a bank account is the most convenient way to manage household finances.

vocab.meaning: cực kỳ đắt đỏ/tốn một khoản tiền khổng lồ
vocab.example: Instead of paying upfront for items that cost a king's ransom, people use credit cards to spread the payments.

vocab.word: look after the pence and the pounds will look after themselves
vocab.ipa: /lʊk ˈæf.tər ðə pens ænd ðə paʊndz wɪl lʊk ˈæf.tər ðəmˈselvz/
vocab.meaning: tích tiểu thành đại (tiết kiệm tiền nhỏ sẽ có tiền lớn)
vocab.example: Automatic savings plans in banks help people follow the principle of looking after the pence, eventually leading to significant wealth.

vocab.meaning: gặp khó khăn về tài chính/phải thắt chặt chi tiêu
vocab.example: When inflation rises and people start to feel the pinch, they often turn to banks for financial restructuring or advice.

vocab.meaning: tạo điều kiện thuận lợi/làm cho mọi việc dễ dàng hơn
vocab.example: The global network of banks helps to smooth the path for international trade and personal remittances across borders.
